hints.to strike: 'To strike' is 'petere' in one sense, but with 'dabitur' it means to ask/request
hints.to think: 'To think' is 'putare' or 'cogitare' — 'petere' means to seek or ask
hints.to push: 'To push' is 'pellere' — 'petere' means to seek or request

hints.present indicative: Present indicative would be 'petitis' — 'Petite' without -is is imperative
hints.present subjunctive: Present subjunctive would be 'petatis' — 'Petite' is imperative
hints.future indicative: Future would be 'petetis' — 'Petite' is present imperative

hints.pono, ponere, posui, positum: These are the parts of 'ponere' (to place)
hints.pello, pellere, pepuli, pulsum: These are the parts of 'pellere' (to drive)
hints.puto, putare, putavi, putatum: These are the parts of 'putare' (to think)

hints.present passive indicative: Present passive would be 'datur' — 'dabitur' with -bi- is future
hints.present subjunctive: Present subjunctive passive would be 'detur' — 'dabitur' is future indicative
hints.perfect passive indicative: Perfect passive would be 'datum est' — 'dabitur' is future

hints.duco, ducere, duxi, ductum: These are the parts of 'ducere' (to lead)
hints.dico, dicere, dixi, dictum: These are the parts of 'dicere' (to say)
hints.doceo, docere, docui, doctum: These are the parts of 'docere' (to teach)

steps.reveal: vobis = to you (dat., indirect object of 'dabitur')

hints.nominative: Nominative would be 'vos' — 'vobis' is dative or ablative
hints.accusative: Accusative would be 'vos' — 'vobis' is dative here
hints.ablative: 'Vobis' can be ablative, but with passive 'dabitur' it is dative recipient

hints.to ask: 'To ask' is 'quaerere' in one sense, but the primary meaning is to seek or search

hints.present indicative: Present indicative would be 'quaeritis' — 'quaerite' is imperative
hints.present subjunctive: Present subjunctive would be 'quaeratis' — 'quaerite' is imperative
hints.perfect indicative: Perfect would be 'quaesivistis' — 'quaerite' is present imperative

hints.queror, queri, questus sum: These are the parts of 'queri' (to complain) — a deponent verb
hints.acquiro, acquirere, acquisivi, acquisitu: These are the parts of 'acquirere' (to acquire) — different compound
hints.requiro, requirere, requisivi, requisitu: These are the parts of 'requirere' (to seek again) — different compound

hints.to invent: While English 'invent' derives from it, Latin 'invenire' primarily means to find or discover
hints.to enter: 'To enter' is 'intrare' or 'ingredi' — 'invenire' means to find

hints.present indicative: Present would be 'invenitis' — 'invenietis' with -ie- is future (4th conj.)
hints.present subjunctive: Present subjunctive would be 'inveniatis' — 'invenietis' is future indicative

hints.venio, venire, veni, ventum: These are the parts of 'venire' (to come) — the base verb
hints.convenio, convenire, conveni, conventum: These are the parts of 'convenire' (to come together)
hints.evenio, evenire, eveni, eventum: These are the parts of 'evenire' (to turn out/happen)

translation: Ask and it will be given to you; seek and you will find
